As of 19 June 2026, there are 7,197,585 articles in the English Wikipedia containing over 5 billion words (giving a mean of about 717 words per article).
Cebuano and Waray are Filipino languages.
At this rate, there are 1573 days until the English Wikipedia reaches 8 million articles.
These numbers come from a screenshot of Special:Statistics at 00:14 UTC on 1 November 2024.
Over time, the percentage of all pages on Wikipedia that consist of articles drops as more non-article pages are created than articles.
The most obvious include the markup text of the articles, templates, media/file descriptions, and primary meta-pages that would be needed to render the text of the latest version of the current encyclopedia proper.
Distribution of the 67,682,566 articles in different language editions (as of 19 June 2026)
Note that Lsjbot, a bot run by Sverker Johansson, is responsible for much of the growth of the second- and fifth-largest Wikipedias, the Cebuano and the Swedish Wikipedias, respectively, as well as the rapid growth of the Waray Wikipedia.
Note: The current mpac3.2 article count for the English-language Wikipedia is 7,197,585 articles.
2007-07-22, 1900000, mpac3.1 (inserted retroactively from data in WA)
2002-11-25, 92777, mpacIII, ~775 bot generated articles added.
In November 2021, the English Wikipedia had 624 words per article, versus 195 words per article for Cebuano, 193 for Swedish, 169 for Dutch, and 117 for Waray-Waray."},{"url":"https://www.wikipedia.org/","title":"Wikipedia","content":"There are no small contributions: every edit counts, every donation counts.
Your support means the world to us."},{"url":"https://diff.wikimedia.org/2026/05/06/the-power-of-collaboration-how-wikigap-2026-is-transforming-the-swahili-wiktionary/","title":"The Power of Collaboration: How “WikiGap 2026” is Transforming the Swahili Wiktionary – Diff","content":"Our goal was clear: push the Swahili Wiktionary toward a historic 80,000 entries.
17,000 Articles Created (Total across projects)
While Wikipedia saw a healthy growth of 1,460 new articles, the Swahili Wiktionary was the real star, receiving 15,600 new entries.
The campaign reached its turning point on April 11, 2026, at the UDSM New Library (Room 221).
Wiktionary Growth: 7,940 new articles created in just one month.
This editorial onslaught resulted in more than 356,000 new words added in Phase II alone.
When we share skills across Arusha, Tabora, and Dar es Salaam, the Swahili language doesn’t just survive online—it thrives."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Main_Page_history/2026_May_7","title":"Wikipedia:Main Page history/2026 May 7 - Wikipedia","content":"She accompanied George Augustus Robinson as a guide on expeditions that resulted in the exile of Tasmania's remaining Aboriginal population; Truganini herself was exiled to the Wybalenna Aboriginal Establishment after the expeditions in 1835."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Page_history","title":"Help:Page history - Wikipedia","content":"For example, the page history for this help page shows it was first created on September 20, 2004, and that it has been changed more than 750 times since then.

For the editing guideline on article size, see Wikipedia:Article size. | **This is an information page.**  It is not a Wikipedia policy or guideline; rather, its purpose is to explain certain aspects of Wikipedia's norms or practices. An image estimating the size of a printed version of the English Wikipedia as of December 2025, including images, based on a random sampling of 500 articles. The **size of the English Wikipedia** can be measured in terms of the number of articles, number of words, number of pages, and the size of the database, among other ways. As of 14 July 2026, there are 7,208,467 articles in the English Wikipedia containing over 5 billion words (giving a mean of about 717 words per article). Wikipedia continues to grow, and the number of articles on Wikipedia is increasing by about 15,000 a month (as of July 2024). In other words, the average article size is growing faster than the number of articles over time.","score":0.09322316,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.98,"request_id":"50931097-e553-4434-9046-276d6f719ead"}
